desc: Sanya is Hainan's tropical resort hub, known for warm water, large beach complexes, and winter-sun demand from across China and wider Asia. Peak pressure is strongest in late autumn through early spring, while summer and early autumn are wetter and more storm-prone but often better value.
hls: Yalong Bay and Haitang Bay resort zones

todo.name: Beach day at Yalong Bay
todo.desc: Swim in clear water and enjoy long sandy stretches with easy resort facilities.

todo.name: Nanshan Cultural Tourism Zone
todo.desc: Visit the giant sea-facing Guanyin statue and temple grounds on Sanya’s southwest coast.

todo.name: Wuzhizhou Island snorkeling
todo.desc: Take a boat for reef snorkeling, sea sports, and bright-blue water conditions.

todo.name: Yalong Bay Tropical Paradise Forest Park
todo.desc: Ride up for canopy walks and elevated views over coastline and resort bays.

todo.name: Haitang Bay duty-free complex
todo.desc: Browse major brands and tax-free retail in one of China’s largest duty-free hubs.

todo.name: Sunset at Coconut Dream Corridor
todo.desc: Stroll the palm-lined waterfront promenade for evening breeze and skyline views.

wifi.note: Resort Wi-Fi is generally stable, but large hotels can slow during evening peaks; remote-work users should verify room-level speeds.

prac.fltNote: No direct Gatwick service; most routings connect via mainland China or regional Asian hubs. Return fares are often ~£480–£760 off-peak, ~£760–£1,050 in shoulder demand, and ~£1,050+ around winter-holiday peaks.
prac.visa: China/Hainan entry eligibility can vary by nationality and policy period; verify current rules before booking.
alerts: Typhoon risk is higher roughly July–October and may affect flights/boat excursions.
Winter holiday peaks can drive sharp rises in accommodation pricing.
Sea conditions can change quickly during storm periods; follow local safety advisories.

sweet: November to April is typically the most reliable beach window; shoulder months can offer better value outside peak holiday dates.
